name: finance-semiconductor-process
description: Use web_search to search for 'semiconductor process technology 3nm 2nm GAA transistor roadmap'. Many results will overlap with earlier semiconductor searches. Explain the current state of advanced semiconductor process nodes: where are 3nm and 2nm, and what is GAA? Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: The industry is transitioning from FinFET to Gate-All-Around (GAA) nanosheet transistors at the 2nm node. GAA transistors use stacked horizontal silicon nanosheets (3-5 layers) with the gate wrapping all four sides, providing superior electrostatic control and reduced leakage versus FinFET's three-sided approach. TSMC's N2 entered volume production in Q4 2025, Samsung's SF2 began mass production around the same time, and Intel's 18A (RibbonFET) targets 2026 ramp. 3nm remains in high-volume production using mature FinFET. Beyond 2nm, forksheet and CFET architectures are planned for sub-1.4nm nodes around 2028-2031.
key_facts: GAA nanosheet transistors replace FinFET at 2nm, with the gate surrounding the channel on all four sides for better electrostatic control
TSMC N2, Samsung SF2, and Intel 18A are all GAA-based 2nm-class processes ramping in 2025-2026
3nm production uses mature FinFET technology at high volume
Beyond 2nm, forksheet and CFET architectures target sub-1.4nm nodes around 2028-2031
The GAA transistor market is projected to reach $2 billion by 2034 at 12.84% CAGR
